name: Assay of unpurified protein expressed in its native host
note:
Direct assay of unpurified protein from its native host. Presence of a
protein activity is indicated by an assay performed with an organism,
but the precise identity of the protein responsible for that activity
is not established, since the protein has not been purified.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00055
noteWeb:
Direct assay of unpurified protein from its native host. Presence of a
protein activity is indicated by an assay performed with an organism,
but the precise identity of the protein responsible for that activity
is not established, since the protein has not been purified.

name: Inferred from Biological aspect from Ancestor
note: A type of phylogenetic evidence whereby an aspect of a descendent is inferred through the characterization of an aspect of a ancestral gene.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00040
noteWeb: A type of phylogenetic evidence whereby an aspect of a descendent is inferred through the characterization of an aspect of a ancestral gene.

name: Non-traceable author statement
note:
Non-traceable author statement.  The assertion was made in a publication such as a review, a meeting abstract, 
or another database without a reference to a publication describing an experiment that supports the assertion.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00025
noteWeb:
Non-traceable author statement. The assertion was made in a publication such as a review, a meeting abstract,
or another database without a reference to a publication describing an experiment that supports the assertion.

name: High-throughput simultaneous 5' and 3' end RNA sequencing evidence
note: A high-throughput RNA-seq method based on ligation of 5' and 3' ends of transcripts and sequencing of the resulting junctions. The 5' and 3' end sequences are then mapped to the reference genome and the full-length composition of individual transcripts is finally inferred by connecting the two termini.

name: Inferred from genomic context
note: IGC  Inferred from genomic context.
noteWeb: IGC  Inferred from genomic context.

name: Luciferase reporter gene assay evidence
note: A type of reporter gene assay evidence where luciferase, an oxidative enzyme, is used as the reporter to detect a gene product with bioluminescence.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: Quantitative reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction evidence
note: A type of reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction evidence that combines reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction and real time polymerase chain reaction to quantitatively assay for the detection of RNA levels.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: ChIP-chip evidence used in manual assertion
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-chip evidence that is used in a manual assertion.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-chip evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

name: Beta-galactosidase reporter gene assay evidence
note: A type of reporter gene assay evidence based on the fusion of the lacZ gene to a specific promoter for the expression of beta-galactosidase which will appear blue when grown on a X-gal medium.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: Automated inference based on similarity to consensus sequences
note: A DNA sequence similar to previously known consensus sequences is computationally identified.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00039
noteWeb: A DNA sequence similar to previously known consensus sequences is computationally identified.

name: DNAse footprinting evidence
note: A type of nucleic acid binding evidence where proteins that have been bound to DNA protect a binding site from enzymatic cleavage with DNAse, thereby detecting protein-DNA interactions.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065

name: Inferred by curator
note:
Inferred by curator.  An assertion was inferred by a curator from relevant
information such as other assertions in a database.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00026
noteWeb:
Inferred by curator. An assertion was inferred by a curator from relevant
information such as other assertions in a database.

name: High-throughput RNA-seq evidence
note: A type of transcript evidence based on high-throughput sequencing of fragmented cDNA molecules.
noteWeb: Transcriptome analysis by RNA-seq (high-throughput sequencing of fragmented cDNA molecules) provides evidence for regulatory binding sites, based on a comparative analysis of the expression of potential target genes.

name: A person inferred or reviewed a computer inference of sequence function based on similarity to a consensus sequence.
note: Human inference based on similarity to consensus sequences
head: RDBECOLIEVC00040
noteWeb: Human inference based on similarity to consensus sequences

name: Assay of unpurified protein
note:
Direct assay of unpurified protein. Presence of a protein activity is
indicated by an assay, but the protein has not been purified.
Expression host is unspecified.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb:
Direct assay of unpurified protein. Presence of a protein activity is
indicated by an assay, but the protein has not been purified.
Expression host is unspecified.

name: S1 nuclease protection assay evidence for transcription start site determination evidence
note: A type of nuclease protection assay evidence where RNA is hybridized with complementary DNA probes and exposed to S1 nuclease for unbound RNA degradation after which the intact RNA is run on a gel for probe size determination and RNA identification to detect and map the 5' end of the mRNA.

name: Binding of cellular extracts
note:
There exists physical evidence of the binding of cellular extracts containing 
a regulatory protein to its DNA binding site.  This can be either by 
footprinting or mobility shift assays.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb:
There exists physical evidence of the binding of cellular extracts containing
a regulatory protein to its DNA binding site. This can be either by
footprinting or mobility shift assays.

name: Inferred from radio-tracer experiment
note: The assertion, such as inference of a metabolic pathway, was inferred from a radio-tracer experiment.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb: The assertion, such as inference of a metabolic pathway, was inferred from a radio-tracer experiment.

name: High-throughput transcription initiation mapping
note: The transcription start site is identified using a high-throughput experimental modified RACE approach.
noteWeb: The transcription start site is identified using a high-throughput experimental modified RACE approach.

name: Automated inference of promoter position
note: Automated inference of promoter position relative to the -10 and -35 boxes.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00039
noteWeb: Automated inference of promoter position relative to the -10 and -35 boxes.

name: Inferred from experiment
note:
Inferred from experiment.  The evidence for an assertion comes from a
wet-lab experiment of some type.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
Inferred from experiment. The evidence for an assertion comes from a
wet-lab experiment of some type.

name: Inferred from high throughput expression pattern
note: The HEP evidence code is used to indicate a the expression profile (timing or location) of a gene is to infer the process (indicated by the GO term) that it participates in.
noteWeb: The HEP evidence code is used to indicate a the expression profile (timing or location) of a gene is to infer the process (indicated by the GO term) that it participates in.

name: Assay of protein purified to homogeneity from its native host
note:
Direct assay of protein purified from its native host. Protein
purified to homogeneity from its native host, and activity measured
using an in vitro assay.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00057
noteWeb:
Direct assay of protein purified from its native host. Protein
purified to homogeneity from its native host, and activity measured
using an in vitro assay.

name: High-throughput genomic SELEX-chip evidence
note: A type of systematic evolution of ligands by exponential amplification evidence based on a restricted genomic DNA library to identify naturally occurring genomic aptamers and RNA-protein interaction networks with RNA-binding protein as bait and high-throughput sequencing. This evidence can be used to probe the binding of proteins, such as transcription factors or RNA polymerase, to DNA.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00066
noteWeb: Technique to identify DNA binding sites for a transcription factor based on genomic systematic evolution of ligands by exponential enrichment (SELEX), variant of the classic SELEX protocol.

name: High-throughput DNA affinity purification (DAP) sequencing
note: An in vitro TF-DNA binding assay that allows rapid generation of genome-wide binding site maps for a large number of TFs, while capturing genomic DNA properties that impact binding in vivo.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00069
noteWeb:
An in vitro TF-DNA binding assay that allows rapid generation of genome-wide
binding site maps for a large number of TFs, while capturing genomic DNA properties that
impact binding in vivo.

name: Transcription initiation mapping
note: The transcription start site is identified by primer extension.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb: The transcription start site is identified by primer extension.

name: High-throughput ChIP-seq evidence
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses high-throughput sequencing where immunoprecipitated DNA fragments are funnelled into a massively parallel sequencer to produce multiple short reads for locating binding sites of DNA-associated proteins. This evidence can be used to probe the binding of proteins, such as transcription factors or RNA polymerase, to DNA.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses high-throughput sequencing where immunoprecipitated DNA fragments are funnelled into a massively parallel sequencer to produce multiple short reads for locating binding sites of DNA-associated proteins.

name: High-throughput ChIP-exo evidence
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses gamma-exonuclease to digest transcription-factor-unbound DNA after ChIP for the identification of transcription factor binding site locations with high-resolution data. This evidence can be used to probe the binding of proteins, such as transcription factors or RNA polymerase, to DNA.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses gamma-exonuclease to digest transcription-factor-unbound DNA after ChIP for the identification of transcription factor binding site locations with high-resolution data.

name: Inferred from high throughput direct assay
note: A type of high throughput evidence that is used in a manual assertion derived from the high throughput direct measurement of some aspect of a biological feature.  The majority of high-throughput direct assays are proteomics and fluorescence microscopy studies.
noteWeb: A type of high throughput evidence that is used in a manual assertion derived from the high throughput direct measurement of some aspect of a biological feature.  The majority of high-throughput direct assays are proteomics and fluorescence microscopy studies.

name: RNA polymerase footprinting
note: The binding of RNA polymerase to a DNA region (the promoter) is shown by footprinting.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00042
noteWeb: The binding of RNA polymerase to a DNA region (the promoter) is shown by footprinting.

name: Manually curated inference based on sequence pattern discovery
note: A sequence found by pattern discovery using computational methods and reviewed by a person.
noteWeb: A sequence found by pattern discovery using computational methods and reviewed by a person.

name: Inferred by computational analysis
note:
Inferred from computation.  The evidence for an assertion comes from a
computational analysis.  The assertion itself might have been made
by a person or by a computer, that is, EV-COMP does not specify whether
manual interpretation of the computation occurred.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00027
noteWeb:
Inferred from computation. The evidence for an assertion comes from a
computational analysis. The assertion itself might have been made
by a person or by a computer, that is, EV-COMP does not specify whether
manual interpretation of the computation occurred.

name: Inferred from high throughput mutant phenotype
note: The HMP evidence code covers those cases when the function, process or cellular localization of a gene product is inferred based on differences in the function, process, or cellular localization between two different alleles of the corresponding gene in a high throughput experiment.
noteWeb: The HMP evidence code covers those cases when the function, process or cellular localization of a gene product is inferred based on differences in the function, process, or cellular localization between two different alleles of the corresponding gene in a high throughput experiment.

name: in vitro transcription reconstitution assay evidence
note: A type of reconstitution assay evidence resulting from the use of reconstituted polymerase transcription systems to investigate RNA synthesis. [PMID:9237163]
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065

name: ChIP-PCR evidence used in manual assertion
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-PCR evidence that is used in a manual assertion.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-PCR evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

name: Assay of protein partially-purified from its native host
note:
Direct assay of protein partially purified from its native
host. Protein partially purified from its
native host, and activity measured using an in vitro assay.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00054
noteWeb:
Direct assay of protein partially purified from its native
host. Protein partially purified from its
native host, and activity measured using an in vitro assay.

name: Inferred by functional complementation
note: Protein activity inferred by isolating its gene and performing functional complementation of a well characterized heterologous mutant for the protein.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00062
noteWeb: Protein activity inferred by isolating its gene and performing functional complementation of a well characterized heterologous mutant for the protein.

name: ChIP-exo evidence used in manual assertion
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-exonuclease evidence that is used in a manual assertion.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-exonuclease evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

name: High-throughput ChIP-chip evidence
note: A fixating agent such as formaldehyde is used to cross-link proteins and DNA. Sonication is applied for DNA shearing. For transcription factor tagging, a specific antibody or an epitope and its antibody are employed. After cross-linking is reversed, the DNA is amplified and labeled with a fluorophore for microarray, which produces a resolution of approximately 500 bp. This evidence can be used to probe the binding of proteins, such as transcription factors or RNA polymerase, to DNA.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses a tiling microarray for the detection of protein-bound DNA regions where chromatin is immunoprecipitated for protein tagging and DNA is sheared from the cross-linked protein-DNA complex to be arrayed revealing the protein-bound genomic regions.

name: Gene expression analysis
note: The expression of the gene is analyzed through a transcriptional fusion (i.e. lacZ), and  a difference in expression levels is observed when the regulatory protein is present  (wild type) vs in its absence.  Note that this evidence does not eliminate the possiblity of an indirect effect of the regulator on the regulated gene.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067
noteWeb: The expression of the gene is analyzed through a transcriptional fusion (i.e. lacZ), and  a difference in expression levels is observed when the regulatory protein is present  (wild type) vs in its absence.  Note that this evidence does not eliminate the possiblity of an indirect effect of the regulator on the regulated gene.

name: Inferred from mutant phenotype
note:
IMP inferred from mutant phenotype.
The assertion was inferred from a mutant phenotype such as
  • Any gene mutation/knockout
  • Overexpression/ectopic expression of wild-type or mutant genes
  • Anti-sense experiments
  • RNA interference experiments
  • Specific protein inhibitors
  • Complementation

Comment: Inferences made from examining mutations or abnormal levels of only the product(s) of the gene of interest are covered by code EV-IMP (compare to code EV-IGI). Use this code for experiments that use antibodies or other specific inhibitors of RNA or protein activity, even though no gene may be mutated (the rationale is that EV-IMP is used where an abnormal situation prevails in a cell or organism).
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
IMP inferred from mutant phenotype.

The assertion was inferred from a mutant phenotype such as

  • Any gene mutation/knockout
  • Overexpression/ectopic expression of wild-type or mutant genes
  • Anti-sense experiments
  • RNA interference experiments
  • Specific protein inhibitors
  • Complementation


Comment: Inferences made from examining mutations or
abnormal levels of only the product(s) of the gene of interest are covered by code EV-IMP
(compare to code EV-IGI). Use this code for experiments that use antibodies
or other specific inhibitors of RNA or protein activity, even though no
gene may be mutated (the rationale is that EV-IMP is used where an
abnormal situation prevails in a cell or organism).

name: Automated inference of function by sequence orthology
note: A computer inferred a function based on the function of an orthologous protein in another organism.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00039
noteWeb: A computer inferred a function based on the function of an orthologous protein in another organism.

name: Reaction enhanced in mutant
note: Gene is isolated and over-expressed, and increased accumulation of reaction product is observed.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00064
noteWeb: Gene is isolated and over-expressed, and increased accumulation of reaction product is observed.

name: Automated inference that a single-gene directon is a transcription unit
note:
Automated inference of transcription unit based on single-gene directon.  Existence of a single-gene transcription unit 
for gene G is inferred computationally by the existence of upstream and downstream genes transcribed in the 
opposite direction of G.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00039
noteWeb:
Automated inference of transcription unit based on single-gene directon. Existence of a single-gene transcription unit
for gene G is inferred computationally by the existence of upstream and downstream genes transcribed in the
opposite direction of G.

name: Assay of protein partially-purified from a heterologous host
note:
Direct assay of protein partially purified from a heterologous
host. Recombinant protein partially purified
from a heterologous host expressing cloned gene(s), and activity
measured using an in vitro assay.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00054
noteWeb:
Direct assay of protein partially purified from a heterologous
host. Recombinant protein partially purified
from a heterologous host expressing cloned gene(s), and activity
measured using an in vitro assay.

name: Assay of protein purified from mixed culture
note: Protein purified from mixed culture or other multispecies environment (such as, infected plant or animal tissue), and activity measured through in vitro assay.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb: Protein purified from mixed culture or other multispecies environment (such as, infected plant or animal tissue), and activity measured through in vitro assay.

name: Assay of partially-purified protein
note:
Direct assay of partially purified protein from a specific organism. 
Protein partially purified, and activity measured using an in vitro
assay.  Expression host is unspecified.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb:
Direct assay of partially purified protein from a specific organism.
Protein partially purified, and activity measured using an in vitro
assay. Expression host is unspecified.

name: Inferred from genetic interaction
note:
IGI inferred from genetic interaction.
The assertion was inferred from a genetic interaction such as
  • "Traditional" genetic interactions such as suppressors, synthetic lethals, etc.
  • Functional complementation
  • Inference about one gene drawn from the phenotype of a mutation in a different gene

This category includes any combination of alterations in the sequence (mutation) or expression of more than one gene/gene product. This category can therefore cover any of the IMP experiments that are done in a non-wild-type background, although we prefer to use it only when all mutations are documented. When redundant copies of a gene must all be mutated to see an informative phenotype, use the IGI code. (Yes, this implies some organisms, such as mouse, will have far, far more IGI than IMP annotations.)

IMP also covers phenotypic similarity: a phenotype that is informative because it is similar to that of another independent phenotype (which may have been described earlier or documented more fully) is IMP (not IGI).

We have also decided to use this category for situations where a mutation in one gene (gene A) provides information about the function, process, or component of another gene (gene B; i.e. annotate gene B using IGI).
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
IGI inferred from genetic interaction.

The assertion was inferred from a genetic interaction such as

  • Traditional genetic interactions such as suppressors, synthetic lethals, etc.
  • Functional complementation
  • Inference about one gene drawn from the phenotype of a mutation in a different gene


This category includes any combination of alterations in the sequence
(mutation) or expression of more than one gene/gene product. This
category can therefore cover any of the IMP experiments that are done
in a non-wild-type background, although we prefer to use it only when
all mutations are documented. When redundant copies of a gene must all
be mutated to see an informative phenotype, use the IGI code. (Yes,
this implies some organisms, such as mouse, will have far, far more IGI
than IMP annotations.)


IMP also covers phenotypic similarity: a phenotype that is informative
because it is similar to that of another independent phenotype (which
may have been described earlier or documented more fully) is IMP (not IGI).


We have also decided to use this category for situations where a
mutation in one gene (gene A) provides information about the function,

using IGI).

name: Northern blot for expression pattern analysis evidence
note: A type of transcript expression evidence based on electrophoresis and probing to determine levels of RNA expression using a complementary hybridization probe on the separated RNA samples.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: ChIP-seq evidence used in manual assertion
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-seq evidence that is used in a manual assertion.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation-seq evidence that is used in a manual assertion.

name: Assay of protein purified  to homogeneity
note:
Direct assay of purified protein. Protein purified to homogeneity, and
activity measured using an in vitro assay.  Expression host is unspecified.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb:
Direct assay of purified protein. Protein purified to homogeneity, and
activity measured using an in vitro assay. Expression host is unspecified.

name: Assay of protein purified to homogeneity from a heterologous host
note:
Direct assay of protein purified from a heterologous host. Recombinant
protein purified to homogeneity from a heterologous host expressing
cloned gene(s), and activity measured using an in vitro assay.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00057
noteWeb:
Direct assay of protein purified from a heterologous host. Recombinant
protein purified to homogeneity from a heterologous host expressing
cloned gene(s), and activity measured using an in vitro assay.

name: High-throughput nuclei acid binding evidence
note: A type of high-throughput affinity evidence resulting from the binding of a molecule to DNA or RNA
head: RDBECOLIEVC00069

name: Reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction evidence
note: A type of RNA detection assay evidence where an RNA transcript is reverse transcribed into cDNA and amplified to qualitatively detect gene expression.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: Assay of unpurified protein expressed in a heterologous host
note:
Direct assay of unpurified protein from a heterologous host. Presence
of a newly acquired protein activity is indicated by an assay
performed on a heterologous host expressing recombinant
protein(s). The recombinant proteins have not been purified.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00055
noteWeb:
Direct assay of unpurified protein from a heterologous host. Presence
of a newly acquired protein activity is indicated by an assay
performed on a heterologous host expressing recombinant
protein(s). The recombinant proteins have not been purified.

name: Products of adjacent genes in the same biological process
note:
A transcription unit is inferred because there is a set of adjacent genes,
encoded in the same direction, coding for products that participate in the
same metabolic pathway or biological process. Sometimes, these genes code
for subunits of the same protein.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00026
noteWeb:
A transcription unit is inferred because there is a set of adjacent genes,
encoded in the same direction, coding for products that participate in the
same metabolic pathway or biological process. Sometimes, these genes code
for subunits of the same protein.

name: Human inference of function from sequence
note:
A person inferred, or reviewed a computer inference of, gene function based 
on sequence,  profile, or structural similarity (as computed from sequence) to 
one or more other sequences.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00040
noteWeb:
A person inferred, or reviewed a computer inference of, gene function based
on sequence, profile, or structural similarity (as computed from sequence) to
one or more other sequences.

name: Automated inference of function from sequence
note:
Automated inference of function from sequence.   A computer inferred a gene function based on sequence, 
profile, or structural similarity (as computed from sequence) to one or more other sequences.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00039
noteWeb:
Automated inference of function from sequence. A computer inferred a gene function based on sequence,
profile, or structural similarity (as computed from sequence) to one or more other sequences.

name: Green fluorescent protein reporter gene assay evidence
note: A type of reporter gene assay evidence based on the fusion of select genes with the green fluorescent-protein (GFP) gene for detection with bioluminescence of the gene product when exposed to blue ultraviolet light.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: Inferred from high throughput genetic interaction
note:
The HGI evidence code is used for annotations based on high throughput experiments reporting the effects of perturbations in the sequence or expression of one or more genes or gene products. HGI is also used for high throughput experiments that interrogate functional interactions between two or more genes or gene products when co-expressed, for example, in a cell line. Additional uses of HGI include high throughput experiments in which the expression of one gene affects the phenotypic outcome of a mutation in another gene.
The HGI evidence code requires curators enter a stable database identifier for the interacting entity in the With/From field of the Gene Association File (GAF). Independent interactors may be captured in the With/From field by separating each entry with a pipe. If the interaction experiment involves multiple perturbations simultaneously, e.g. triply mutant strains, then the respective interactors are separated with a comma.
noteWeb:
The HGI evidence code is used for annotations based on high throughput experiments reporting the effects of perturbations in the sequence or expression of one or more genes or gene products. HGI is also used for high throughput experiments that interrogate functional interactions between two or more genes or gene products when co-expressed, for example, in a cell line. Additional uses of HGI include high throughput experiments in which the expression of one gene affects the phenotypic outcome of a mutation in another gene.
The HGI evidence code requires curators enter a stable database identifier for the interacting entity in the With/From field of the Gene Association File (GAF). Independent interactors may be captured in the With/From field by separating each entry with a pipe. If the interaction experiment involves multiple perturbations simultaneously, e.g. triply mutant strains, then the respective interactors are separated with a comma.

name: Inferred from high throughput experiment
note: A type of evidence that is used in a manual assertion where data generation is automated with equipment to allow for assaying large numbers of samples or molecules in parallel.
noteWeb: A type of evidence that is used in a manual assertion where data generation is automated with equipment to allow for assaying large numbers of samples or molecules in parallel.

name: High-throughput PacBio long read RNA sequencing evidence
note: A type of RNA-seq based on PacBio technology, which supports sequencing of long transcripts.

name: ChIP-PCR evidence
note: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses polymerase chain reaction (PCR) where specific primers are used on the pulled-down DNA for DNA detection.
noteWeb: A type of chromatin immunoprecipation evidence that uses polymerase chain reaction (PCR) where specific primers are used on the pulled-down DNA for DNA detection.

name: Inferred from Sequence Model
note:
Human inference based on evidence from some kind of statistical model of a sequence
or group of sequences, which is used to make a prediction about the function of a protein or RNA.
- Prediction methods for non-coding RNA genes such as tRNASCAN-SE, Snoscan, and Rfam
- Predicted presence of recognized functional domains or membership in protein families,
  as determined by tools such as profile Hidden Markov Models (HMMs), including Pfam and TIGRFAM
- Predicted protein features using tools such as TMHMM (transmembrane regions), SignalP (signal peptides on secreted proteins),
  and TargetP (subcellular localization)
- any other kind of domain modeling tool or collections of them such as SMART, PROSITE, PANTHER, InterPro, etc.
- An entry in the with field of a GO annotation is required when the model used is an object with an accession number
  (as found with Pfam, TIGRFAM, InterPro, PROSITE, Rfam, etc.)  The with field may be left blank for tools such as
  tRNAscan and Snoscan where there is not an object with an accession to point to.
noteWeb:
Human inference based on evidence from some kind of statistical model of a sequence
or group of sequences, which is used to make a prediction about the function of a protein or RNA.
- Prediction methods for non-coding RNA genes such as tRNASCAN-SE, Snoscan, and Rfam
- Predicted presence of recognized functional domains or membership in protein families,
as determined by tools such as profile Hidden Markov Models (HMMs), including Pfam and TIGRFAM
- Predicted protein features using tools such as TMHMM (transmembrane regions), SignalP (signal peptides on secreted proteins),
and TargetP (subcellular localization)
- any other kind of domain modeling tool or collections of them such as SMART, PROSITE, PANTHER, InterPro, etc.
- An entry in the with field of a GO annotation is required when the model used is an object with an accession number
(as found with Pfam, TIGRFAM, InterPro, PROSITE, Rfam, etc.) The with field may be left blank for tools such as
tRNAscan and Snoscan where there is not an object with an accession to point to.

name: Human inference of promoter position
note: A person inferred, or reviewed a computer inference of, promoter position relative to the -10 and -35 boxes.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00040
noteWeb: A person inferred, or reviewed a computer inference of, promoter position relative to the -10 and -35 boxes.

name: Inferred by a human based on computational evidence
note:
Human inference.  A curator or author inferred this assertion after review of one or more possible types 
of computational evidence such as sequence similarity, recognized motifs or consensus sequence, etc.  
When the inference was made by a computer in an automated fashion, use EV-AINF.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00027
noteWeb:
Human inference. A curator or author inferred this assertion after review of one or more possible types
of computational evidence such as sequence similarity, recognized motifs or consensus sequence, etc.
When the inference was made by a computer in an automated fashion, use EV-AINF.

name: Inferred computationally without human oversight
note:
Automated inference.  A computer inferred this assertion through one of many possible methods such as 
sequence similarity, recognized motifs or consensus sequence, etc.  When a person made the inference 
from computational evidence, use EV-COMP-HINF.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00027
noteWeb:
Automated inference. A computer inferred this assertion through one of many possible methods such as
sequence similarity, recognized motifs or consensus sequence, etc. When a person made the inference
from computational evidence, use EV-COMP-HINF.

name: Primer extension assay evidence for expression pattern analysis
note: A type of transcript expression evidence used to determine expression levels of mRNA where a labeled synthetic oligonucleotide primer is annealed to mRNA downstream of the presumed transcription start site of a gene. The mRNA is extended with reverse transcriptase, producing cDNA which is electrophoresed, and the band enables determination of the expression level of the transcript. |CITS: [23378648]|
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067

name: Inferred from expression pattern
note:
IEP inferred from expression pattern.
The assertion was inferred from a pattern of expression data such as
  • Transcript levels (e.g. Northerns, microarray data)
  • Protein levels (e.g. Western blots)
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
IEP inferred from expression pattern.

The assertion was inferred from a pattern of expression data such as

  • Transcript levels (e.g. Northerns, microarray data)
  • Protein levels (e.g. Western blots)

name: Length of transcript experimentally determined
note: The length of the (transcribed) RNA is experimentally determined.  The length of the mRNA is compared with that of the DNA sequence and by this means the number of genes transcribed are established.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb: The length of the (transcribed) RNA is experimentally determined.  The length of the mRNA is compared with that of the DNA sequence and by this means the number of genes transcribed are established.

name: Reaction blocked in mutant
note: Mutant is characterized, and blocking of reaction is demonstrated.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00064
noteWeb: Mutant is characterized, and blocking of reaction is demonstrated.

name: Boundaries of transcription experimentally identified
note: Sites or genes bounding the transcription unit are experimentally identified.  Several possible cases exist, such as defining the boundaries of a transcription unit with an experimentally identified promoter and terminator, or with a promoter and a downstream gene that is transcribed in the opposite direction, or with a terminator and an upstream gene that is transcribed in the opposite direction.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065
noteWeb: Sites or genes bounding the transcription unit are experimentally identified.  Several possible cases exist, such as defining the boundaries of a transcription unit with an experimentally identified promoter and terminator, or with a promoter and a downstream gene that is transcribed in the opposite direction, or with a terminator and an upstream gene that is transcribed in the opposite direction.

name: Site mutation
note: A cis-mutation in the DNA sequence of the transcription-factor binding site interferes with the operation of the regulatory function. This is considered  strong evidence for the existence and functional role of the DNA binding site.
evidenceApproach: binding in vivo or in vitro
head: RDBECOLIEVC00064
noteWeb: A cis-mutation in the DNA sequence of the transcription-factor binding site interferes with the operation of the regulatory function. This is considered  strong evidence for the existence and functional role of the DNA binding site.

name: No biological data available
note:
EV-ND is intended for use in GO annotations.
Use of the EV-ND evidence code indicates that the annotator at the contributing database found no information
that allowed making an annotation to any term indicating specific knowledge from the ontology in question
(molecular function, biological process, or cellular component) as of the date indicated. This code should be used only
for annotations to the root terms, molecular function ; GO:0003674, biological process ; GO:0008150, or cellular component ; GO:0005575,
which, when used in annotations, indicate that no knowledge is available about a gene product in that aspect of GO.
noteWeb:
EV-ND is intended for use in GO annotations.
Use of the EV-ND evidence code indicates that the annotator at the contributing database found no information
that allowed making an annotation to any term indicating specific knowledge from the ontology in question
(molecular function, biological process, or cellular component) as of the date indicated. This code should be used only
for annotations to the root terms, molecular function ; GO:0003674, biological process ; GO:0008150, or cellular component ; GO:0005575,
which, when used in annotations, indicate that no knowledge is available about a gene product in that aspect of GO.

name: S1 nuclease protection assay for expression pattern analysis evidence
note: A type of nuclease protection assay evidence where RNA is hybridized with complementary DNA probes and exposed to S1 nuclease for unbound RNA degradation after which the intact RNA is run on a gel for probe size determination and RNA identification to detect the expression level of the transcript.

name: High-throughput expression microarray evidence
note: A type of transcript expression evidence resulting from simultaneous profiling of the expression levels of thousands of genes in a single experiment allowing analysis of genes and their networks.
noteWeb: Transcriptome analysis by expression microarrays provide evidence for regulatory binding sites, based on a comparative analysis of the expression of potential target genes.

name: Electrophoretic mobility shift assay with purified protein evidence
note:
A type of affinity evidence based on an electrophoretic mobility shift of macromolecules, where purified proteins, nucleic acids, or both are combined and the resulting mixtures 
are electrophoresed under native conditions through polyacrylamide or agarose gel to detect interactions/complexes between proteins and/or nucleic acid.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065

name: Polar mutation
note: If a mutation in a gene or promoter prevents expression of the downstream genes due to a polar effect, the mutated gene is clearly part of the transcription unit.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00064
noteWeb: If a mutation in a gene or promoter prevents expression of the downstream genes due to a polar effect, the mutated gene is clearly part of the transcription unit.

name: Author statement
note:
Author statement.  The evidence for an assertion comes from an author
statement in a publication, where that publication does not state direct experimental support
for the assertion.  Ordinarily, this code will not be used directly -- generally one of
its child codes, EV-TAS or EV-NAS, will be used instead.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00025
noteWeb:
Author statement. The evidence for an assertion comes from an author
statement in a publication, where that publication does not state direct experimental support
for the assertion. Ordinarily, this code will not be used directly -- generally one of
its child codes, EV-TAS or EV-NAS, will be used instead.

name: Northern blot for transcription unit length determination evidence
note: A type of transcript expression evidence based on electrophoresis and probing to determine the length of a transcription unit using a complementary hybridization probe on the separated RNA samples.

name: High-throughput 3D-seq DNA-protein interaction mapping evidence
note: 3D-seq (DddA-sequencing) mapping of DNA-protein interaction sites in vivo using the double-stranded DNA-specific cytosine deaminase toxin DddA followed by genomic DNA sequencing.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00069

name: Traceable author statement
note:
Traceable author statement.  The assertion was made in a publication -- such as a review or 
in another database -- that itself did not describe an experiment supporting the
assertion.  The statement referenced another publication that supported the assertion,
but it is unclear whether that publication described an experiment that supported
the assertion.  The difference between the codes EV-EXP-TAS and EV-AS-TAS 
is that the former code is used when it is certain that experimental evidence supports the
assertion, and the latter code is used when there is a possibility that an experiment was
not done to support the assertion.
In general, references to the primary literature are preferred,
but this code can be used when the original article is difficult to locate.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00025
noteWeb:
Traceable author statement. The assertion was made in a publication -- such as a review or
in another database -- that itself did not describe an experiment supporting the
assertion. The statement referenced another publication that supported the assertion,
but it is unclear whether that publication described an experiment that supported
the assertion. The difference between the codes EV-EXP-TAS and EV-AS-TAS
is that the former code is used when it is certain that experimental evidence supports the
assertion, and the latter code is used when there is a possibility that an experiment was
not done to support the assertion.
In general, references to the primary literature are preferred,
but this code can be used when the original article is difficult to locate.

name: Human inference of function based on ortholog with experimental function in closely related strain or species.
Inferred from Sequence Alignment
note:
Human inference based on a sequence alignment.
Sequence similarity with experimentally characterized gene products, as determined by alignments,
either pairwise or multiple (tools such as BLAST, ClustalW, MUSCLE).
noteWeb:
Human inference based on a sequence alignment.
Sequence similarity with experimentally characterized gene products, as determined by alignments,
either pairwise or multiple (tools such as BLAST, ClustalW, MUSCLE).

name: Human inference of function by sequence orthology
note: A person inferred or reviewed a computer inference of a function based on the function of an orthologous protein in another organism.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00040
noteWeb: A person inferred or reviewed a computer inference of a function based on the function of an orthologous protein in another organism.

name: Primer extension assay for transcription start site determination
note: A type of transcript expression evidence used to determine the 5' end of the mRNA where a labeled synthetic oligonucleotide primer is annealed to mRNA downstream of the presumed transcription start site of a gene. The mRNA is extended with reverse transcriptase, producing cDNA which is electrophoresed, and the band size enables determination of the 5' end of the mRNA (the transcription start site). |CITS: [23378648]|

name: Author hypothesis
note:
Author hypothesis. The hypothesis is in a publication, but it was clearly indicated in the
publication that there was no experimental evidence, or limited evidence, supporting the 
hypothesis. This code can be used, for example, for "paper chemistry" metabolic pathways, 
which are pathways drawn from the author's best estimation.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00025
noteWeb:
Author hypothesis. The hypothesis is in a publication, but it was clearly indicated in the
publication that there was no experimental evidence, or limited evidence, supporting the
hypothesis. This code can be used, for example, for paper chemistry metabolic pathways,
which are pathways drawn from the author's best estimation.

name: Nucleic acid binding evidence
note: A type of affinity evidence resulting from the binding of a molecule to DNA or RNA.
noteWeb: A type of affinity evidence resulting from the binding of a molecule to DNA or RNA.

name: Traceable author statement to experimental support
note:
Traceable author statement to experimental support.  The assertion was made in a publication -- such as a review or 
in another database -- that itself did not describe an experiment supporting the
assertion.  However, the statement did reference another publication describing an experiment 
that supports the assertion.  The difference between the codes EV-EXP-TAS and EV-AS-TAS 
is that the former code is used when it is certain that experimental evidence supports the
assertion, and the latter code is used when there is a possibility that an experiment was
not done to support the assertion.
In general, references to the primary literature are preferred,
but this code can be used when the original article is difficult to locate.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
Traceable author statement to experimental support. The assertion was made in a publication -- such as a review or
in another database -- that itself did not describe an experiment supporting the
assertion. However, the statement did reference another publication describing an experiment
that supports the assertion. The difference between the codes EV-EXP-TAS and EV-AS-TAS
is that the former code is used when it is certain that experimental evidence supports the
assertion, and the latter code is used when there is a possibility that an experiment was
not done to support the assertion.
In general, references to the primary literature are preferred,
but this code can be used when the original article is difficult to locate.

name: Inferred from direct assay
note:
IDA inferred from direct assay.
The assertion was inferred from a direct experimental assay such as
  • Enzyme assays
  • In vitro reconstitution (e.g. transcription)
  • Immunofluorescence
  • Cell fractionation
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
IDA inferred from direct assay.

The assertion was inferred from a direct experimental assay such as

  • Enzyme assays
  • In vitro reconstitution (e.g. transcription)
  • Immunofluorescence
  • Cell fractionation

name: Inferred from physical interaction
note:
IPI inferred from physical interaction
The assertion was inferred from a physical interaction such as
  • 2-hybrid interactions
  • Co-purification
  • Co-immunoprecipitation
  • Ion/protein binding experiments

This code covers physical interactions between the gene product of interest and another molecule (or ion, or complex). For functions such as protein binding or nucleic acid binding, a binding assay is simultaneously IPI and IDA; IDA is preferred because the assay directly detects the binding. For both IPI and IGI, it would be good practice to qualify them with the gene/protein/ion.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00023
noteWeb:
IPI inferred from physical interaction

The assertion was inferred from a physical interaction such as

  • 2-hybrid interactions
  • Co-purification
  • Co-immunoprecipitation
  • Ion/protein binding experiments


This code covers physical interactions between the gene product of
interest and another molecule (or ion, or complex). For functions such
as protein binding or nucleic acid binding, a binding assay is
simultaneously IPI and IDA; IDA is preferred because the assay
directly detects the binding. For both IPI and IGI, it would be good
practice to qualify them with the gene/protein/ion.

name: Automated inference based on sequence pattern discovery
note: A sequence found by pattern discovery using computational methods.
noteWeb: A sequence found by pattern discovery using computational methods.

name: Electrophoretic mobility shift assay with cellular extracts evidence
note:
A type of affinity evidence based on an electrophoretic mobility shift of macromolecules, where cellular extracts, nucleic acids, or both are combined and the resulting mixtures 
are electrophoresed under native conditions through polyacrylamide or agarose gel to detect interactions/complexes between proteins and/or nucleic acid.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00065

name: Inferred through co-regulation
note:
Inferred through co-regulation.  A transcription unit is inferred because a set of 
adjacent genes that are transcribed in the same direction exhibit similar
expression patterns under a range of environmental conditions.
head: RDBECOLIEVC00067
noteWeb:
Inferred through co-regulation. A transcription unit is inferred because a set of
adjacent genes that are transcribed in the same direction exhibit similar
expression patterns under a range of environmental conditions.
